session.user:
FIRST check docs/learned/index.md for existing documentation on this topic. Read relevant docs before exploring raw files. Then explore codebase as necessary.
Read these source files and report key patterns:
1. `packages/erk-dev/src/erk_dev/commands/audit_collect/command.py` - Overall structure, JSON output schema, ErkDevContext fields used 2. `src/erk/cli/commands/slot/assign_cmd.py` - Look at lines 16-20 for `--from-current-branch` flag definition, and lines 52-79 for target branch selection logic (Graphite parent vs trunk fallback, detached HEAD) 3. `src/erk/tui/screens/objective_nodes_screen.py` - Look at lines 150-151 for priority=True binding pattern 4. `src/erk/tui/commands/registry.py` - Look at alphabetical sorting within command groups 5. `src/erk/tui/screens/help_screen.py` - Look at lines 59-119 for ViewMode-aware conditional rendering
Report exact function names, line numbers, and key patterns.
session.assistant: I'll search the documentation first, then read the specified source files to identify key patterns.

tool_use.name: Grep
tool_use.param: command structure|JSON schema|CLI flag|binding pattern|conditional rendering

1→--- 2→title: CLI Flag Patterns 3→read_when: 4→ - "designing CLI flag requirements" 5→ - "implementing conditional flag requirements" 6→ - "documenting flag combinations" 7→last_audited: "2026-02-16 14:20 PT" 8→audit_result: clean 9→--- 10→ 11→# CLI Flag Patterns 12→ 13→Patterns for CLI flag design, including asymmetric requirements and flag combinations. 14→ 15→## Asymmetric Flag Requirements 16→ 17→Some flags are only required in certain contexts or combinations. Document these clearly. 18→ 19→### Pattern: Flag Required Unless Config Disables 20→ 21→The `--dangerous` flag is required by default but can be disabled via config: 22→ 23→```python 24→@click.command() 25→@click.option("-d", "--dangerous", is_flag=True) 26→@click.pass_obj 27→def my_command(ctx: ErkContext, *, dangerous: bool) -> None: 28→ # Require --dangerous unless config disables requirement 29→ if not dangerous: 30→ require_flag = (

1→--- 2→title: Click Patterns 3→last_audited: "2026-02-03 03:56 PT" 4→audit_result: edited 5→read_when: 6→ - "implementing CLI options with complex behavior" 7→ - "creating flags that optionally accept values" 8→ - "designing CLI flags with default behaviors" 9→--- 10→ 11→# Click Patterns 12→ 13→Advanced patterns for Click option and flag behavior. 14→ 15→## Optional Value Flags with Defaults 16→ 17→Use `is_flag=False` with `flag_value` to create flags that work both with and without values: 18→ 19→```python 20→@click.option( 21→ "--environment", 22→ type=str, 23→ default=None, # None when flag not provided 24→ is_flag=False, # Accepts a value 25→ flag_value="", # Empty string when flag provided without value 26→ help="Use environment (uses default if name not provided)", 27→) 28→``` 29→ 30→**Behavior:**
... [70 lines omitted] ...

1→--- 2→title: TUI Command Registration 3→read_when: 4→ - "adding a new TUI command to the registry" 5→ - "understanding the 3-place coordination pattern for TUI commands" 6→ - "working with TUI command categories or display formatters" 7→tripwires: 8→ - action: "adding a new TUI command without updating all 3 places" 9→ warning: "TUI commands require 3-place coordination: registry definition, display formatter, and action inventory. See tui-command-registration.md." 10→ - action: "adding a CLI flag that affects behavior without checking TUI command palette" 11→ warning: "TUI command palette generates shell commands via src/erk/tui/commands/registry.py. When adding CLI flags that change behavior, check if TUI-generated commands need the flag too." 12→--- 13→ 14→# TUI Command Registration 15→ 16→Adding a TUI command requires coordinating three places in the codebase. 17→ 18→## 3-Place Coordination Pattern 19→ 20→When adding a command, update all three: 21→ 22→1. **Display formatter**: A `_display_*()` function that generates the display name 23→2. **CommandDefinition**: Entry in `get_all_commands()` with id, name, category, availability, and display getter 24→3. **Action inventory**: Command appears in the correct category 25→ 26→All definitions are in `src/erk/tui/commands/registry.py`. 27→ 28→## Single Source of Truth: `launch_key` Field 29→ 30→<!-- Source: src/erk/tui/commands/types.py:60 -->
... [75 lines omitted] ...

1→"""Slot assign command - assign an existing branch to a worktree slot.""" 2→ 3→import click 4→ 5→from erk.cli.commands.slot.common import allocate_slot_for_branch 6→from erk.cli.core import discover_repo_context 7→from erk.cli.ensure import Ensure 8→from erk.core.context import ErkContext 9→from erk.core.repo_discovery import ensure_erk_metadata_dir 10→from erk_shared.output.output import user_output 11→ 12→ 13→@click.command("assign") 14→@click.argument("branch_name", metavar="BRANCH", required=False, default=None) 15→@click.option("-f", "--force", is_flag=True, help="Auto-unassign oldest branch if pool is full") 16→@click.option( 17→ "--from-current-branch", 18→ is_flag=True, 19→ help="Move the current branch to a pool slot and switch the current worktree away", 20→) 21→@click.pass_obj 22→def slot_assign( 23→ ctx: ErkContext, branch_name: str | None, force: bool, from_current_branch: bool 24→) -> None: 25→ """Assign an EXISTING branch to an available pool slot. 26→ 27→ BRANCH is the name of an existing git branch to assign to the pool. 28→ 29→ The command will: 30→ 1. session.tool_result:
1→"""Command registry for command palette. 2→ 3→This module defines all available commands and their availability predicates. 4→Commands are organized by category: Actions, Opens, Copies. 5→Plan commands appear only in Plans/Learn views; objective commands appear only in Objectives view. 6→""" 7→ 8→from erk.tui.commands.types import CommandCategory, CommandContext, CommandDefinition 9→from erk.tui.views.types import ViewMode 10→ 11→CATEGORY_EMOJI: dict[CommandCategory, str] = { 12→ CommandCategory.ACTION: "⚡", 13→ CommandCategory.OPEN: "🔗", 14→ CommandCategory.COPY: "📋", 15→} 16→ 17→ 18→# === View Mode Predicates === 19→ 20→ 21→def _is_plan_view(ctx: CommandContext) -> bool: 22→ """True when not in Objectives view (i.e., Plans or Learn).""" 23→ return ctx.view_mode != ViewMode.OBJECTIVES 24→ 25→ 26→def _is_objectives_view(ctx: CommandContext) -> bool: 27→ """True when in Objectives view.""" 28→ return ctx.view_mode == ViewMode.OBJECTIVES 29→ 30→

session.assistant:
Excellent. Now let me compile the report with all the key patterns found.
## Report: Key Patterns from Source Files
Based on analysis of the specified source files and relevant documentation, here are the exact patterns and functions:
---
### 1. **audit_collect/command.py** - JSON Output Schema & Command Structure
**File:** `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-dev/src/erk_dev/commands/audit_collect/command.py`
**JSON Output Schema (Lines 118-135):** - Top-level: `AuditResult` (frozen dataclass, lines 127-135) - `success: bool` - `summary: AuditSummary` (lines 118-124) - `total_local_branches`, `total_worktrees`, `total_open_prs` - `categories: AuditCategories` (lines 106-115) - Contains nested frozen dataclasses: `blocking_worktrees`, `auto_cleanup`, `closed_pr_branches`, `pattern_branches`, `stale_open_prs`, `needs_attention`, `active` - `stubs_tracked_by_graphite: list[str]`
**Command Function (Lines 433-496):** - Function name: `audit_collect_command` (line 435) - Decorated: `@click.command(name="audit-collect")` and `@click.pass_context` (lines 433-434) - Takes: `ctx: click.Context` → extracts `ErkDevContext` via `ctx.obj` (lines 441-444) - Output: Uses `click.echo(json.dumps(asdict(audit_result), indent=2))` (line 495) - Exit: `raise SystemExit(0)` (line 496)
**ErkDevContext Fields Used (Lines 441-444):** ```python dev_ctx: ErkDevContext = ctx.obj git = dev_ctx.git # Gateway to git operations github = dev_ctx.github # Gateway to GitHub operations repo_root = dev_ctx.repo_root # Repository root Path ```
**Core Logic Function (Lines 371-430):** - `_run_audit_collect()` separated for testability - Takes pre-fetched data (branches, worktrees, sync_info, PRs) - Returns `AuditResult` with `success=True` - Categorization is side-effect-free
---
### 2. **slot/assign_cmd.py** - CLI Flag & Branch Selection Logic
**File:** `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/slot/assign_cmd.py`
**Flag Definition (Lines 16-20):** ```python @click.option( "--from-current-branch", is_flag=True, help="Move the current branch to a pool slot and switch the current worktree away", ) ```
**Target Branch Selection Logic (Lines 52-79):**
1. **Get current branch (Line 53-55):** ```python current_branch = Ensure.not_none( ctx.git.branch.get_current_branch(ctx.cwd), "Unable to determine current branch" ) ```
2. **Determine preferred target (Line 58-59):** ```python parent_branch = ctx.branch_manager.get_parent_branch(repo.root, current_branch) to_branch = parent_branch or ctx.git.branch.detect_trunk_branch(repo.root) ``` **Pattern:** Graphite parent as priority, trunk as fallback
3. **Handle detached HEAD (Lines 71-77):** ```python checkout_path = ctx.git.worktree.is_branch_checked_out(repo.root, to_branch) if checkout_path is not None: ctx.branch_manager.checkout_detached(ctx.cwd, current_branch) else: ctx.branch_manager.checkout_branch(ctx.cwd, to_branch) ``` **Pattern:** If target branch in use elsewhere → detached HEAD checkout; otherwise → normal checkout
---
### 3. **objective_nodes_screen.py** - Priority=True Binding Pattern
**File:** `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/tui/screens/objective_nodes_screen.py`
**Binding Lines (150-151):** ```python Binding("down", "cursor_down", "Down", show=False, priority=True), Binding("up", "cursor_up", "Up", show=False, priority=True), ```
**Full BINDINGS List (Lines 144-156):** - Simple key aliases (vim-style `j`/`k` as `show=False`) - Arrow keys marked with `priority=True` to take precedence over vim-style - Pattern: `Binding(key, action, description, show=False, priority=True)` for high-priority navigation
---
### 4. **tui/commands/registry.py** - Alphabetical Sorting Within Categories
**File:** `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/tui/commands/registry.py`
**Function Structure (Lines 208-222):** ```python def get_all_commands() -> list[CommandDefinition]: """Return all command definitions.
Commands are ordered by category: 1. Actions (mutative operations) 2. Opens (browser navigation) 3. Copies (clipboard operations) """ return [ # === PLAN ACTIONS === CommandDefinition(id="close_plan", ...), CommandDefinition(id="dispatch_to_queue", ...), # ... more actions ...
# === OBJECTIVE ACTIONS === CommandDefinition(id="check_objective", ...), # ... more actions ...
# === PLAN OPENS === CommandDefinition(id="open_issue", ...), # ... more opens ...
# === PLAN COPIES === CommandDefinition(id="copy_checkout", ...), # ... more copies ... ] ```
**Organizational Pattern:** - Commands grouped by **category header comments** (PLAN ACTIONS, OBJECTIVE ACTIONS, PLAN OPENS, etc.) - Within categories: Roughly grouped by function (e.g., all PLAN ACTIONS together) - **Not strictly alphabetized**, but logically organized by feature area
**Category Predicates (Lines 21-28):** ```python def _is_plan_view(ctx: CommandContext) -> bool: return ctx.view_mode != ViewMode.OBJECTIVES
def _is_objectives_view(ctx: CommandContext) -> bool: return ctx.view_mode == ViewMode.OBJECTIVES ```
**Display Name Functions Pattern (Lines 34-160+):** - Each command has a `_display_*()` function by name convention - Example: `_display_close_plan()` (line 34-36) - Returns fully formatted display string (e.g., `f"erk pr close {ctx.row.plan_id}"`)
---
### 5. **tui/screens/help_screen.py** - ViewMode-Aware Conditional Rendering
**File:** `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/tui/screens/help_screen.py`
**ViewMode Detection (Lines 59-69):** ```python def __init__(self, *, view_mode: ViewMode) -> None: """Initialize help screen.""" super().__init__() self._view_mode = view_mode
def _is_objectives_view(self) -> bool: return self._view_mode == ViewMode.OBJECTIVES ```
**Conditional Rendering Pattern (Lines 71-121):** ```python def compose(self) -> ComposeResult: """Create help dialog content.""" is_objectives = self._is_objectives_view()
# ... static sections ...
with Vertical(classes="help-section"): yield Label("Actions", classes="help-section-title") if is_objectives: yield Label("Enter View objective details", classes="help-binding") yield Label("Ctrl+P Commands (opens detail modal)", classes="help-binding") yield Label("v View objective text", classes="help-binding") # ... objective-specific bindings ... else: yield Label("Enter View plan details", classes="help-binding") yield Label("Ctrl+P Commands (opens detail modal)", classes="help-binding") yield Label("v View plan text", classes="help-binding") # ... plan-specific bindings ... ```
**Pattern Details:** - Store `view_mode` as instance variable in `__init__` - Create helper method `_is_objectives_view()` for clarity - Use simple `if is_objectives:` in `compose()` to branch rendering - Objective section (Lines 94-99), Plan section (Lines 100-108) - Shared sections rendered outside conditional (Lines 79-91, 112-121)
---
### Summary Table
| File | Key Function | Pattern | Line Range | |------|--------------|---------|-----------| | audit_collect/command.py | `audit_collect_command` | Click context injection, JSON output via `asdict()` | 433-496 | | assign_cmd.py | `slot_assign` | CLI flag, Graphite parent→trunk fallback, detached HEAD handling | 16-77 | | objective_nodes_screen.py | BINDINGS | `priority=True` on arrow keys for navigation precedence | 150-151 | | registry.py | `get_all_commands()` | Logical category grouping, display functions by convention | 208-500+ | | help_screen.py | `compose()` | ViewMode instance var + conditional rendering in generator | 59-121 |
